vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: This is a standard STIG hardening for session timeouts in logind, which is an auth-related configuration. It involves a simple config file edit with minimal risk of system breakage or lockout, following recommended security practices.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; low complexity; no critical service impact

# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10
#HandlePowerKey=poweroff
#HandlePowerKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleRebootKey=reboot
#HandleRebootKeyLongPress=poweroff
#HandleSuspendKey=suspend
#HandleSuspendKeyLongPress=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKey=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleLidSwitch=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchExternalPower=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchDocked=ignore
#PowerKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The finding is about configuring logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity. The rule name is "logind_session_timeout" and it references the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf.

According to the description, I need to:
1. Edit /etc/systemd/logind.conf
2. Ensure there is a [Login] section
3. Set the StopIdleSessionSec= parameter

Let me first read the current configuration file to see what's there.
reasoning_messages.content: I'll help you remediate this OpenSCAP finding. Let me start by reading the current logind configuration file to understand what needs to be modified.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The configuration file exists and has a [Login] section. I need to add the StopIdleSessionSec= directive to terminate idle sessions after a certain time. According to the rule, I need to configure logind to terminate inactive user sessions after a certain time. The remediation should set this value appropriately.

Let me check what the standard value should be. Based on typical security practices, idle session timeouts are often set to 15 minutes (900 seconds) for inactivity. However, I should look at what the OpenSCAP rule expects. Since the finding is about configuring this setting, I need to set it to a reasonable value.

Let me review the plan with the expected value.
